h2h_summary: DR Congo and Uzbekistan have never previously met in any official international fixture, making this their first-ever competitive encounter.
key_players_away: Eldor Shomurodov (captain, striker, Istanbul Basaksehir — 44 international goals)
team_style_home: DR Congo deploy a compact 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1, emphasising defensive organisation and explosive counter-attacks through wide forwards with exceptional pace.
team_style_away: Under Fabio Cannavaro, Uzbekistan use a disciplined 3-4-2-1 built on Italian-style defensive compactness and sharp counter-attacking bursts through Shomurodov and Fayzullaev.
wc_history_home: DR Congo (then Zaire) made their sole World Cup appearance in 1974, becoming the first Sub-Saharan African side at the tournament but losing all three group games and conceding 14 goals; 2026 marks their dramatic return after 52 years.
wc_history_away: Uzbekistan are making their first-ever FIFA World Cup appearance in 2026, qualifying as AFC runners-up in a historic achievement as the first Central Asian nation to reach the tournament.
group_context: Group K is headlined by Portugal and Colombia, widely expected to take the top two spots, leaving DR Congo and Uzbekistan competing fiercely for a third-place berth that could still advance under the expanded 48-team format; a win here would be decisive for either side's knockout-round hopes.
strengths_home: Explosive counter-attacking pace through Wissa and Mbuku on the wings
Defensive leadership and experience through captain Mbemba (107 caps)
Strong physical presence and aggressive pressing in midfield
strengths_away: Prolific and experienced captain in Shomurodov, all-time top scorer with 44 goals
Premier League defensive quality via Manchester City's Khusanov
Disciplined defensive structure and dangerous from set pieces
